Article.ArticleTitle: A thermostable, cellulase-free xylanase from newly isolated Peniophora incarnata ST09: Purification, characterization and ultrasound-assisted activity enhancement.

Abstract.AbstractText: This study reports the production, purification, optimization and biochemical characterization of a thermostable, cellulase-free xylanase from a newly isolated fungal strain, Peniophora incarnata ST09. Xylanase production was carried out using solid-state fermentation and optimized with respect to pH, temperature, moisture content and incubation period. Maximum enzyme production was achieved at pH 5.0, 55 °C, 70% moisture content and 7 days incubation. The enzyme was purified using ion-exchange (DEAE-cellulose) and gel filtration (Sephadex G-100) chromatography. It was purified 11.8-fold with a yield recovery of 62.78% and a specific activity of 27.71 U/mg. SDS-PAGE confirmed enzyme homogeneity with estimated molecular weight of ∼36 kDa. The purified xylanase exhibited maximum activity at 50 °C and pH 5.0 and retained significant storage stability up to 30 days at 4 °C. Enzyme activity was enhanced by Mg2+ and Ca2+ but inhibited by Cu2+ and Ag+ ions. Substrate specificity demonstrated strong affinity toward beechwood xylan and arabinoxylan with no detectable activity toward Avicel, starch and carboxymethyl cellulose, confirming its cellulase-free nature. This property is an essential trait for industrial processes such as paper and pulp processing where cellulase activity can be detrimental. Kinetic analysis revealed a Km of 6.7 mg/mL and Vmax of 685.3 μmol/mg/min. Furthermore, a single ultrasound treatment (33 kHz, 10 min) enhanced xylanase production (390 U/mL) compared to the control (307 U/mL), whereas repeated treatments (10, 20 and 30 h) declined enzyme yield. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first report of cellulase-free xylanase from Peniophora spp., highlighting its potential in industrial biotechnology applications.
